# Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ: FRMI) — News & Price-Impact Summary

**Window June 3–10, 2026 · FRMI ~$5.62 (Jun 9 close) → intraday ~$7.10 on Jun 10, roughly +25% · Net read: a quiet, drifting-lower week capped by a sharp OpenAI-driven spike, all against the backdrop of an unresolved boardroom war.**

## What's moving the stock

The dominant catalyst hit on **June 10, 2026**, when an analyst note from **Citizens (JMP)** tied Fermi to reports that **OpenAI is exploring a roughly 10-gigawatt data-center lease in Ohio, backed by Nvidia financing**. Citizens framed the development as a positive read-through for Fermi, arguing it positions the company as one of the fastest available routes to large-scale power and compute, and reinforced its view that OpenAI is likely among the counterparties evaluating capacity at Fermi's **Project Matador** campus in Amarillo, Texas — an 11 GW behind-the-meter natural-gas and power complex designed to scale far larger over time. Citizens has carried a Market Outperform rating with a $30 price target. The mechanism is straightforward: Fermi is a pre-revenue developer whose entire equity thesis hinges on landing a first anchor tenant, so any signal that a hyperscaler the size of OpenAI is in the funnel is treated as existential good news.

The price reaction was real and same-session. FRMI closed **June 9 at $5.62** (down about 1.4% on the day). On **June 10** it gapped up and ran, with intraday prints reported around **$6.35 (+13%)**, then higher toward **$6.99–$7.50 (+25% to +27%)**, against a prior close of $5.62 and an intraday high near $7.10. Volume was heavy and call-option activity was flagged as unusually active and directionally bullish. Because this was a stock-specific catalyst — a single-name analyst note keyed to AI-infrastructure headlines, not a broad market melt-up — the move is best attributed to the Fermi/OpenAI story rather than macro drift.

What's at stake is whether the optimism converts into a **binding tenant agreement**. Management's "Fermi 2.0" plan has publicly targeted securing binding tenant commitments within a 90-day window, and the company has pointed to roughly $1.4 billion already invested in infrastructure, over 2 GW of secured generation, and new equipment financing. The OpenAI headlines are, for now, third-party reports and analyst inference — not a signed lease — so the catalyst is anticipatory. A confirmed contract would likely validate the spike; a stretch of silence or a tenant going elsewhere would expose the stock to giving it back, especially given a 52-week range that runs from about $4.47 up to roughly $37 and an IPO price of $21 in October 2025.

Layered underneath everything is an **unresolved governance fight** that keeps a discount on the shares. Ousted co-founder and former CEO **Toby Neugebauer** — terminated for cause on April 17, 2026 and Fermi's largest shareholder with around 139 million shares — is running a proxy campaign to reconstitute the board and pursue a sale or strategic partnership, with a shareholder-called **special meeting anticipated on or around June 30, 2026**. The board, backed by second-largest holder Caddis Capital (~9.3%), is fighting him and has characterized his push as an attempt to force a sale at a depressed valuation. This overhang is the reason a credible tenant signal produces outsized upside: the market is pricing significant uncertainty about both commercial execution and who controls the company.

## Other developments

**Insider sale by Chief Nuclear Construction Officer.** A Form 4 disclosed that **Mesut Uzman** sold **158,541 shares on June 3, 2026** at a weighted average of about **$6.31**, totaling roughly **$1.0 million**, with part of the sale executed automatically as a "sell-to-cover" for vesting taxes. He retained about 1.34 million shares (≈670,968 direct and 670,491 indirect via spouse). Coverage framed it as his first open-market sale and as liquidity management rather than a directional signal, and there was no clean isolated price move attributable to it. *Source: StockTitan / Investing.com / Motley Fool · Jun 3–7, 2026 · minor, no confirmed move.*

**Proxy machinery continues.** Fermi filed proxy materials on **June 3, 2026** as part of the contest with Neugebauer, and on **June 9** Neugebauer announced he had posted fresh material to LinkedIn and X (dated June 8) reiterating his case for a shareholder vote and a value-maximizing sale or partnership. These keep the June 30 special-meeting fight front-of-mind but did not produce a distinct positive price reaction during the window. *Source: StockAnalysis / Simply Wall St · Jun 3, 8–9, 2026 · sentiment overhang, no confirmed move.*

**Financial backdrop.** The most recent reported results (Q1 2026, released May 14, 2026, just outside the window) showed a **net loss of about $189 million**, driven largely by non-cash share-based compensation, with revenue below forecasts — a reminder that this is a development-stage story trading on future power/compute optionality, not current earnings. *Source: Investing.com / StockAnalysis · May 14, 2026 · context, pre-window.*

## Quick reference

| Date | Headline | Impact |
|------|----------|--------|
| Jun 3 | Chief Nuclear Construction Officer Uzman sells 158,541 shares (~$1.0M) | Minor / no confirmed move |
| Jun 3 | Fermi files proxy materials in contest with ex-CEO | Overhang / no confirmed move |
| Jun 8–9 | Neugebauer posts new shareholder material (LinkedIn/X) ahead of ~Jun 30 special meeting | Overhang / no confirmed move |
| Jun 9 | FRMI closes ~$5.62, down ~1.4% | Mild drift lower |
| Jun 10 | Citizens flags OpenAI's 10 GW Ohio lease talks (Nvidia-backed) as positive for Fermi | CONFIRMED: ~+25% intraday surge |

## What to watch

Net read: a thin, drifting week was overwhelmed by a single AI-infrastructure catalyst that sent FRMI up roughly 25% on June 10, but the move rests on third-party reports and analyst inference — not a signed deal — while an unresolved control fight caps how much the market will trust it.

- **ANTICIPATED — special meeting on or around June 30, 2026**: the shareholder vote that could decide board control and the company's strategic direction (independence vs. sale/partnership).
- **ANTICIPATED — first binding tenant agreement**: management's "Fermi 2.0" plan has pointed to securing binding tenant commitments within ~90 days; any confirmed lease (especially involving OpenAI) would be the validating catalyst.
- **ANTICIPATED — follow-through on OpenAI/Nvidia reporting**: confirmation or denial of OpenAI's interest in Fermi capacity.

**Single most important thing to watch:** whether the OpenAI optimism converts into an actual signed anchor-tenant lease — that, more than the proxy fight or analyst notes, is what turns this spike into a durable re-rating.
